content.full_text:
seiu    United Service Employees Local 616, AFL-CIO
337 17th Street, #204, Oakland, CA 94612 • (510) 452-2366 • FAX 452-2436

April 14, 1992


Fair Political Practices Commission
Attention: Legal Division
28 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Commissioners:

This letter is in regard to the conflict of interest codes within county government.

Local 616 of Service Employees International Union represents professional and clerical employees within the Alameda County Assessor's Office. Recently a new conflict of interest code was adopted and we wanted to follow up with questions to your legal division.

1. Who governs what qualifies a classification to be under the conflict of interest code? For example our appraisers do not make decisions within the Assessor's Office. Our understanding of the law is that it covers people who make decisions (policy makers and/or people who implement them).

2. Does your office maintain all county "conflict of interest" codes? If so, could you send our office some sample language, i.e., Los Angeles, San Francisco, Sacramento.

3. Could your office review the attached language proposed by the Assessor's office? Does it exceed the state law?

4. A Report of Outside Employment. How many other counties in California use this type of form? Is this a part of the law? Can you send us sample forms?

Your assistance in this matter is greatly appreciated. Also anything that you can add that will help us understand both Section 672 and 1365 would also be appreciated.

Sincerely,

Cliff Leo Tillman, Jr.
FIELD REPRESENTATIVE

Enclosures

CLT:gec
opeiu/29/afl-cio

Service Employees International Union

OFFICE OF ASSESSOR PERSONNEL MANUAL                                    4.01 1/7


                        4.01 - CONFLICT OF INTEREST

A.  General

    All Assessor's Office employees are subject to the Conflict of Interest

    provisions established by state law.  Because of the nature of our work

    and the sensitivity of information we obtain from taxpayers, we require

    strict compliance with these provisions.  The purpose of these laws is to

    protect the integrity of the Assessor's Office and to avoid any perception

    of impropriety.

    Section 2-1.21 of the Alameda County Administrative Code provides that

    "No...employee who receives compensation as such shall have any other

    employment or engage in any other activity, which employment is, by law or

    in fact, incompatible with his employment by the County."

B.  Revenue & Taxation Code

    1.  Section 672 - Section 672 requires that all certified appraisers and

        auditor-appraisers disclose his/her financial interest in any

        corporation.  The filing of the Statement of Economic Interest in

        accordance with our Conflict of Interest Code (See 4.01 C. below)

        satisfies this requirement.

    2.  Section 1365 - Section 1365 provides that:

        a.  The County Assessor and the employees of the Assessor's Office

            shall not engage in any gainful profession, trade, business or

            occupation whatsoever for any person, firm, or corporation, or

            be so engaged in their own behalf which profession, trade,

            business or occupation is incompatible or involves a conflict of

            interest with their duties as officers and employees of the

            county.


P - 7/88
R - 3/92

OFFICE OF ASSESSOR PERSONNEL MANUAL                                4.01 2/7

4.01 - CONFLICT OF INTEREST (cont'd.)

B.    Revenue & Taxation Code (cont'd.)

      2.  Section 1365 (cont'd.)

          a. (cont'd.) Conflict of Interest shall include receipt of

compensation or gifts from private persons or firms for advice or

other services relating to the taxation or assessment of property.

          b.    (Reference to Assessor only.)

          c.    If the Assessor or the Attorney General finds that any of the

employees of the Assessor's Office has violated any provision

of subdivision (a), such violation shall be grounds for

dismissal (removal) of such employee by the Assessor.

      3.  Prohibited Activities - The following activities are incompatible

with employment in the Assessor's Office and therefore, are

prohibited:

          a.    receiving money or gifts from private persons or firms for

advice or other services relating to the taxation or

assessment of property;

          b.    making an appraisal or assisting another in making an

appraisal for a fee or gift involving property located in

Alameda County;

          c.    engaging in the sale, trade, or transfer of property located

in Alameda County for a fee or gift.  Purchasing or selling

property for the employee's own use (including

income-producing property) and not for resale is excluded;

          d.    purchasing property located in Alameda County and sold at a

delinquent property tax sale;


D - 7/88
R - 3/92

OFFICE OF ASSESSOR PERSONNEL MANUAL                                4.01 3/7

4.01 - CONFLICT OF INTEREST (cont'd.)

B.    Revenue & Taxation Code (cont'd.)

      3. Prohibited Activities (cont'd.)

         e.    employment as an appraiser or in appraisal-related activities

              by a firm engaged in the appraisal or sale of property located

              in Alameda County.

         f.    unauthorized use of confidential information prepared or kept

              by the Assessor's Office for private gain or advantage or the

              furnishing of confidential information to any person not

              authorized to inspect such information;

         g.    preparing or assisting in the preparation of federal or state

              income tax returns for a fee or gift for any person or firm

              who operates a business located in Alameda County;

         h.    providing accounting, auditing, bookkeeping, taxconsulting or

              similar services for any person or firm who operates a

              business in Alameda County;

         i.    the services described in paragraphs (g) and (h) do not

              include rendering of services on behalf of a church, lodge,

              social, or employee organization for a nominal fee and of

              which the employee is a member. Further, it does not include

              rendering of services for a business in which the employee or

              a member of his or her immediate family is the sole proprietor

              or holds a substantial (50% or more) interest as a partner or

              stockholder unless the business offers services in accounting,

              auditing, bookkeeping, or tax consulting;

         j.    "Property" as used in this section means assessable property.




P - 7/88
R - 3/92

OFFICE OF ASSESSOR PERSONNEL MANUAL                                    4.01 4/7

4.01 - CONFLICT OF INTEREST (cont'd.)

B.    Revenue & Taxation Code (cont'd.)

      4.  All certified appraisers and auditor-appraisers must file an

          annual report of outside employment activities (see Attachment

          A) to insure compliance with Revenue & Taxation Code, Section

          1365.  The report is to be filed no later than January 31st of

          the calendar year for which the report covers.

C.    Government Code, Sections 81000 et. seq.


                         CONFLICT OF INTEREST CODE FOR THE
                    OFFICE OF ASSESSOR, COUNTY OF ALAMEDA


The Political Reform Act, Government Code Section 81000, et seq., requires
state and local government agencies to adopt and promulgate conflict of
interest codes. The Fair Political Practices Commission has adopted a
regulation, 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section 18730, which contains the terms of a
standard conflict of interest code. It can be incorporated by reference and
may be amended by the Fair Political Practices Commission after public notice
and hearings to conform to amendments in the Political Reform Act. Therefore,
the terms of 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section 18730 and any amendments to it duly
adopted by the Fair Political Practices Commission are hereby incorporated by
reference¹ and, along with the attached Appendix in which members and
employees are designated and disclosure categories are set forth, constitute
the conflict of interest code of the Office of Assessor.

The persons holding positions listed in the appendix are Designated
Employees. It has been determined that these persons make or participate in
the making of decisions which may foreseeably have a material effect on
financial interests. Designated employees shall file their Statements of
Economic Interests with the Secretary II, Office of Assessor, who will make
the statements available for public inspection and reproduction (Government
Code Section 81008).


___________________________________________________________________________

¹Copies of Section 18730 are retained by the Chief Deputy Assessor,
Administrative Services Officer, and the Assessor's Secretary II and are
available for reference between 8:30 a.m. and 5 p.m. for any Assessor's
employee or members of the general public.




P - 7/88
R - 7/91

OFFICE OF ASSESSOR PERSONNEL MANUAL                                    4.01 5/7

4.01 - CONFLICT OF INTEREST (cont'd.)

D.    Appendix

                                                                           1 of 2

                              A P P E N D I X

                    C O N F L I C T   O F   I N T E R E S T

                  OFFICE OF ASSESSOR, COUNTY OF ALAMEDA

                            Designated Positions

CHIEF DEPUTY ASSESSOR.......................................................0023 M

ADMINISTRATIVE SERVICES OFFICER.............................................0226 M

CHIEF, APPRAISAL DIVISION....................................................0645 M

PRINCIPAL, APPRAISAL SERVICES................................................2630 M

PRINCIPAL, BUSINESS PERSONAL PROPERTY.......................................2725 M

PRINCIPAL, REAL PROPERTY APPRAISAL...........................................2630 M

SUPERVISING APPRAISER I.......................................................2623 M

SUPERVISING APPRAISER II......................................................2625 M

SUPERVISING AUDITOR-APPRAISER II.............................................2720 M

TRAINING OFFICER, ASSESSOR....................................................2624 M

EXEMPTION SUPERVISOR..........................................................2820 M

APPRAISER I....................................................................2610

APPRAISER II...................................................................2615

APPRAISER III..................................................................2620

REAL PROPERTY APPRAISER AIDE..................................................2604

AUDITOR-APPRAISER I...........................................................2705

AUDITOR-APPRAISER II..........................................................2710

AUDITOR-APPRAISER III.........................................................2715

ASSESSMENT ROLL SUPERVISOR....................................................2875 M

SUPERVISING CLERK II, ASSESSMENT ROLL......................................1139 M

CONSULTANTS....................................................................  *

_________________________________________________________________________________
P - 7/91

OFFICE OF ASSESSOR PERSONNEL MANUAL                                    4.01 6/7

4.01 - CONFLICT OF INTEREST (cont'd.)

D. Appendix (cont'd.)

                                                                          2 of 2




Note: 1. The Assessor is subject to the provisions of Government Code

         Section 87200 et seq. and therefore not governed by this code.

    2. All of the designated positions listed above are in a disclosure

       category requiring the reporting of: all sources of income;

       interests in real property; investments and business positions in

       business entities.


*Consultants shall be included in the list of designated employees and shall

disclose pursuant to the broadest disclosure category in the code subject to

the following limitation.


The Assessor may determine in writing that a particular consultant, although a

"designated position," is hired to perform a range of duties that is limited

in scope and thus is not required to fully comply with the disclosure

requirements in this section. Such written determination shall include a

description of the consultant's duties and, based upon that description, a

statement of the extent of disclosure requirements. The Assessor's

determination is a public record and shall be retained for public inspection

in the same manner and location as this conflict of interest code.




P - 7/91

ANNUAL EMPLOYEE REPORT OF OUTSIDE EMPLOYMENT ACTIVITIES

Calendar Year _______

Employee Name: _________________________________________________________________
(Please Print)        (Last)                    (First)                (Initial)

Job Title:        _________________________________________________________________

     I certify that I have read and understand the Assessor's Conflict of
Interest Policy and that if I am employed/self-employed, such employment
is not incompatible with this policy and does not include any of the
following activities:


     1.  Providing advice or other services relating to the taxation or
         assessment of property to private persons or firms for compensation or
         gifts.

     2.  Making any appraisals or assisting others in making any appraisals for
         compensation or gifts involving property located in Alameda County, or
         employment by a firm engaged in the appraisal or sale of property
         located in Alameda County.

     3.  Engaging in the sale, trade or transfer of property located in Alameda
         County for a fee or gift, or purchasing property located in Alameda
         County and sold at delinquent property tax sales.

     4.  Assisting in the preparation of Federal or State income tax returns or
         providing accounting, auditing, bookkeeping, tax consulting or similar
         services for a fee or gift for any person or firm who provide such
         services for businesses located in Alameda County.




__________________ __________________________________________________
     (Date)                            (Employee Signature)




111-1001-164 (New 3/92)
